summary: boardstep preinstall fetches and silently executes remote.exe dropper
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall hook (`node install.js`, declared in package.json line 10) downloads https://www.pooron.org/tester.exe to a randomized filename in the OS temp directory, chmods it to 0755, and spawns it with `{ detached: true, stdio: 'ignore', windowsHide: true }` followed by `child.unref()` (install.js line 9 hardcodes `PAYLOAD_URL`; install.js lines 60-65 perform the detached spawn). There is no hash or signature verification, errors are swallowed, and the binary executes across Windows/macOS/Linux. The advertised purpose is a 'kanban board utility', but index.js is a 12-line stub with no kanban functionality and the package author field is a keyboard-mash placeholder ('sfhbdrffthger') — the package has no legitimate function and exists solely to deliver the remote payload. The destination domain pooron.org has no relation to the package's stated purpose. This is a textbook install-time dropper executing attacker-controlled bytes on every installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
